description: Synthetic retrieval tasks: one gold memory per case among distractors, with paraphrased queries (LoCoMo / long-mem-eval style, local-only).

memories.content: The CI matrix runs Node 20 and Node 22 on ubuntu-latest.
Flaky tests were quarantined in the e2e suite last sprint.
When editing the installer, run MCP integration tests before claiming done.
The replay job stores offsets in LanceDB under the memories table.
Provider retries are capped at three attempts with exponential backoff.
The staging cluster uses a separate JUSTMEMORY_HOME for dogfood runs.
The retry bug only reproduces when provider retries overlap with the replay job.
Release notes for 0.0.1 mention local ONNX embeddings only.
The explore TUI uses ink and React 18.

queries: When does the flaky retry issue show up relative to replay?
overlap between replay job and provider retries
Does the retry bug need both retries and replay?

memories.content: HTTP transports for MCP exist but are not the default for this repo.
Claude Desktop expects JSON-RPC over stdio for MCP servers.
Workspace-scoped install writes under .cursor when requested.
Use MCP stdio for local JustMemory clients; avoid background daemons.
The CLI entrypoint is dist/cli.js after build.
Profiles map workspace paths to profile_id rows.
Audit events record memory_recall with candidate counts.

queries: How should editors talk to JustMemory on this machine?
stdio versus HTTP for the memory MCP server

memories.content: Ingest batches default to 32 rows per transaction.
Embedding download runs in pretest via download-embedding-model.mjs.
Vector search filters on indexing_state ready and status active.
Lexical recall tokenizes on alphanumeric chunks of length two or more.
Session handoff writes task memories with open verification items.
Verify whether async ingest resumes after MCP restart; not confirmed yet.
LanceDB migrations assert store writes before mutating schema.
Redaction state defaults to none for new memories.

queries: What is still unknown about ingest after a restart?

memories.content: User prefers dark mode in the IDE theme.
User prefers 2-space indentation in TypeScript.
User prefers pnpm over npm for this monorepo.
User prefers running vitest with fileParallelism disabled.
User prefers vim-style modal editing and hjkl navigation in editors.
User prefers zsh as the default shell on Linux workstations.
User prefers English for commit messages and PR titles.
User prefers conventional commits with chore and feat prefixes.

queries: What editing style does the user want in the editor?

memories.content: Monday standup moved to 10:00 UTC for the memory workstream.
Tuesday spike focused on LanceDB vector column distances.
On Monday the investigation ruled out timezone parsing as the root cause.
Wednesday review covered recall fusion weights.
Thursday patch adjusted lexical versus vector blend constants.
Friday demo highlighted citation IDs inside context blocks.

queries: What did we conclude about time zones in the bug hunt?
was tz parsing the culprit Monday

memories.content: Memory recall merges lexical scores with vector distance when embeddings exist.
Memory recall orders candidates by combined lexical and vector signals.
Memory recall adds a small boost when memory_type is instruction.
Memory recall uses topic_key overlap when present on records.
Memory recall returns candidate_ids aligned with merged ranking.
Memory recall exposes retrieval_channels_used for debugging.
Memory recall surfaces why_retrieved strings for transparency.
Memory recall applies recency boost using updated_at age in days.

queries: Which signal uses updated_at age for ranking memories?
how does freshness enter merged recall scoring
